name: Orzo and Protabellow Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon capers (or to taste--this recipe is pretty loose, it's all to taste)
Salt and crushed red pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Put water on to boil for the orzo.
2. Reconstitute sundried tomatoes in a bowl with about a cup of water (rinse them off first; you will use the soaking water later).
3. Wash portabellos and cut off stems. Place caps in a dish, sprinkle with the three herbs, and pour the cup of wine over them to marinate.
4. Coarsely chop mushroom stems and mince garlic.
5. In a nonstick pan, sauté garlic and mushroom stems in a little tomato water or wine.
6. Drain tomatoes, reserving the liquid, and chop coarsely (it's okay if they are not fully softened at this point). Add to the pan and continue sautéing.
7. When the orzo is boiling, add it to cook.
8. Scoot the tomato/garlic/chopped mushroom mixture to the edges of the pan and place mushroom caps in the middle.
9. Pour the reserved tomato water over the mushrooms and cover. Simmer until mushrooms are finished (about 12 minutes, same time as orzo cooking).
10. Remove mushroom caps to plates. Add capers to the pan and cook, stirring, until reduced.
11. Drain orzo and toss with the mixture.
12. Serve with mushroom caps.
